marketState: neutral, with a cautious bullish near-term tilt from weather demand
summary: Front-month sits at $2.942, essentially at model fair value ($2.923) and well inside a wide $2.42-$3.42 band, so there is no meaningful valuation mispricing today. The strongest actionable signal is weather: cooling demand is running +16.5% above the 5-year norm in the 8-16 day window (rangePos 0.76) and rising fast (+21% vs 5y normal, +14.9 in 24h), a genuine near-term bullish catalyst that also lifts the fair-value curve toward $3.02 by +21D. That is offset by a widening storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y, this week's build beating forecast by +22 Bcf bearish) and a 16-year seasonality read that is explicitly low-reliability and only tepid (38% odds of a higher price 3-4 weeks out, typical 4-week move -1.4%). Managed money is near a 5-year extreme net-short (8th percentile), a contrarian bullish setup for a squeeze, while technicals show bullish short-term (5m/15m) momentum sitting inside an intact daily/weekly downtrend (RSI 39-42, price below the 200 EMA) with the 4h stretched above its upper Bollinger band. Data-quality caveat: front price is not live and the futures curve and production data are missing from this snapshot, so curve-based and technical conclusions carry extra uncertainty.

longTerm.rationale: The top-priority seasonality signal is only tepidly bullish and explicitly flagged Low reliability (38% odds higher at 3-4 weeks, typical 4-week move -1.4%, 59pp 10-90 spread), so it offers no durable edge either way. Bullish 8-16D weather demand and an 8th-percentile managed-money net-short (contrarian bullish) are counterbalanced by a growing storage surplus (+180 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish +22 Bcf surprise) and an intact daily/weekly downtrend, leaving no dominant directional edge to size a multi-week position around.
longTerm.invalidation: A larger-than-forecast storage build alongside weather normalizing back to 5y-normal demand would confirm the bearish case and rule out a long; a sharp COT short-covering move without a weather or storage catalyst would argue against chasing a squeeze-driven long.
longTerm.trigger: Wait for either (a) a smaller-than-forecast storage print or managed-money short-covering that confirms the demand story, (b) a pullback toward the lower fair-value band (~$2.60-2.70) to buy value, or (c) the 3-4 week probHigher reading moving back above 50%.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk, reduce to ~0.5% given 4h overextension
shortTerm.rationale: Both weather windows are running hot vs 5y normal (demand_1_7D +3.4%, demand_8_16D +16.5%, rangePos 0.76) with the trend accelerating (+14.9 in 24h, +21% vs 5y), and 5m/15m technicals are bullish (EMA 50>200, positive MACD) with front price sitting right at/slightly above fair value. This favors a small tactical long into the demand catalyst.
shortTerm.invalidation: A close back below $2.85 or weather demand cooling back toward 5y-normal would invalidate the near-term long thesis.

keyDrivers: Weather demand +16.5% vs 5y in the 8-16 day window and accelerating - bullish near-term catalyst
Storage surplus widening (+180 Bcf vs 5y) with a bearish +22 Bcf build surprise this week
Managed money net-short at a 5y 8th percentile - contrarian bullish, squeeze risk
Seasonality is tepid and low-reliability: only 38% odds of higher price 3-4 weeks out
Front price essentially at fair value ($2.942 vs $2.923 fair) - no valuation edge either direction
